text: Welcome to The Long View. Today we're exploring the risk profile of self experimentation in depth with Dr. Elena Fischer, researcher in metabolic and longevity science.
Thanks, Alex Morgan. This is a topic I've spent decades on.

The short answer is Self-experimentation requires a control group of one, and that control is your own boring baseline. Teams working with WHOOP run into this constantly. The framing matters because it tells you which trade-offs you're actually making.
